title: UML Class Diagram — Library Management System
examTip: Class Diagrams model system structure by showing classes, attributes, methods, and relationships. Generalization (inheritance) shows specialized classes pointing to their base class (e.g. Librarian and Member pointing to User). In flowchart representations, use boxes with fields separated by lines (representing Class Name, Attributes, and Methods) and label associations clearly.
theory:
A UML Class Diagram is a static structure diagram that describes the structure of a system by showing the system's classes, their attributes, operations (or methods), and the relationships among objects.

Classes and Fields: The Library Management System contains several key domain classes:
1. User (Base class): Holds general credentials (userId, name, email) and authorization method (login).
2. Librarian (Subclass): Inherits from User and includes administrative functions (addBook, removeBook, verifyMember).
3. Member (Subclass): Inherits from User and tracks checkout metrics (memberId, barcode, totalBooksCheckedOut).
4. Book: Persists catalogue details (isbn, title, author, isReferenceOnly) and provides availability checks.
5. BookLending (Transaction): Manages borrowing details (creationDate, dueDate, returnDate) and transactional methods (renewBook, collectFine).
6. Catalog: Aggregates catalog operations (totalBooks) and provides search interfaces.

Relationships: Generalization (inheritance) is used to represent that Librarian and Member are types of Users. Associations illustrate operational dependencies: Librarians manage Books; the Catalog contains Books; Members initiate lending transactions; and lending transactions borrow Books.
keyPoints: Class Diagrams provide a static view of the system, helping developers structure object models
Generalization represents object-oriented inheritance (Librarian/Member inheriting from User)
Attributes define object properties (e.g. isbn), while methods define class behaviors (e.g. isAvailable)
Associations (lines with arrows) capture connections: Catalog contains Books, lending references Book
UML class diagrams are mapped to database structures (tables) and classes during codebase implementation
useCases: Generating object-oriented code structures in languages like Java, C++, or Python
Designing database schemas based on entity attributes and relations
Communicating software architectures and class responsibilities to developers
mermaid_code:
flowchart TD
  class_User["User
- userId: String
- name: String
- email: String
--
+ login: boolean"]:::umlClass
  class_Librarian["Librarian
- staffId: String
--
+ addBook: void
+ removeBook: void
+ verifyMember: void"]:::umlClass
  class_Member["Member
- memberId: String
- barcode: String
- totalBooksCheckedOut: int
--
+ getBooksCheckedOut: int"]:::umlClass
  class_Book["Book
- isbn: String
- title: String
- author: String
- isReferenceOnly: boolean
--
+ isAvailable: boolean"]:::umlClass
  class_Lending["BookLending
- creationDate: Date
- dueDate: Date
- returnDate: Date
--
+ renewBook: boolean
+ collectFine: void"]:::umlClass
  class_Catalog["Catalog
- totalBooks: int
--
+ searchByTitle: List
+ searchByAuthor: List"]:::umlClass

  %% Inheritance
  class_Librarian -->|"inherits"| class_User
  class_Member -->|"inherits"| class_User

  %% Associations
  class_Librarian -->|"manages"| class_Book
  class_Catalog -->|"contains"| class_Book
  class_Member -->|"requests"| class_Lending
  class_Lending -->|"borrows"| class_Book
